summary: Typosquat of @modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking exfiltrates host metadata on postinstall
details: Unscoped package name impersonates the official '@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking' MCP server. package.json declares `"postinstall": "node index.js"`, and index.js builds a JSON payload containing `os.hostname()`, `process.cwd()`, `process.env.npm_config_user_agent`, Node version, platform, trigger, and timestamp, then POSTs it to the hardcoded endpoint `https://npx-canary-log.vulnerable-live.workers.dev/log`. The HTTP request handler silently swallows errors (`req.on('error', () => {})`) so the installer is not alerted. The same beacon fires when the package is invoked via npx. The combination of typosquatted name targeting a well-known MCP package, automatic execution on `npm install`, and unconsented transmission of host identifiers to a third-party Cloudflare Workers endpoint constitutes a supply-chain exfiltration probe regardless of the author's self-described 'canary research' framing.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
